summary: Spanish font glyphs: accent-fold + built N-tilde and inverted ?/!
detail: [I18N 2026-07-22] Accented vowels are folded to their base vowel at render time (fold_accent_cp in td5_font.c, applied at all 6 font entry points; covers menu/hud/title), so a e i o u show without accents in the normal font. menu.ttf (MontBlanc Trial, whose accented outlines are watermark junk) was patched by re/tools/build_es_menu_font.py: ntilde/Ntilde rebuilt from the clean n/N + a tilde transplanted from hud.ttf, and exclamdown/questiondown built by rotating the clean !/? 180deg. Result: no Spanish glyph uses the arialbd fallback anymore. Confirm: OPTIONS -> LANGUAGE -> ESPANOL (AR) then look at (1) hub reads OPCIONES / OPCIONES GRAFICAS / MENU PRINCIPAL with NO accent marks and one consistent typeface; (2) LANGUAGE value ESPANOL (AR) shows a proper N-with-tilde matching the menu font; (3) exit confirm buttons SI / iNO! show the inverted exclamation; (4) a screen with an opening question mark (e.g. a back-confirm / post-race what-next) shows the inverted ?; (5) nothing renders in a visibly different fallback font. Font is force-added past the re/assets gitignore; re-run the build script to retune tilde placement (NTILDE/CAPTILDE consts).

summary: Spanish (es-AR) localization + LANGUAGE options screen
detail: [I18N 2026-07-21] Full runtime-switchable Argentinian-Spanish localization: new td5_i18n.c catalog (re/assets/frontend/lang/es_AR.txt, UTF-8 KEY=VALUE decoded to Latin-1 at load), all 144 SNK_* labels TR()-wrapped via the regenerated td5_snk_strings.h, ~280 inline literals wrapped across frontend/HUD/pause/tutorial/carstats/net/MP-setup, menu-font accent support (watermark scan widened to Latin-1 + cp>=128 fallback), Latin-1 TOUPPER replacing C toupper in draw paths, and fe_fit_text_scale condensing over-wide translated captions. New LANGUAGE screen (46) under the OPTIONS hub with an ENGLISH/ESPANOL (AR) selector; persists as [Game] Language / --Language=N; switch applies live. Confirm: (1) OPTIONS -> LANGUAGE -> ESPANOL (AR): hub + every options screen + main menu + quick race + race options + track/car select + lobby read Spanish with correct accents (no tofu/TRIAL marks), (2) in-race HUD shows VUELTA/TIEMPO/1RO..6TO/CONTRAMANO/META, pause shows PAUSA/CONTINUAR/REINICIAR CARRERA (no accents by design), (3) switch back to ENGLISH live and relaunch remembers the choice, (4) missing-catalog fallback: rename es_AR.txt and the game boots in English cleanly, (5) known English leftovers (race-type description paragraphs, credits role headers, car-stat sub-captions) are follow-up catalog work flagged by i18n miss lines in frontend.log.

summary: RACE OPTIONS is now the single game-behaviour screen for every mode (GAME OPTIONS removed)
detail: [CONSOLIDATION 2026-07-21] The GAME OPTIONS screen (13) was deleted and its rows folded into the dynamic RACE OPTIONS screen (44), which every mode reaches from its track-select (button 3) and which is inserted as a pre-launch step for modes with no track-select. RACE OPTIONS now carries: OPPONENTS, TRAFFIC, POLICE, DIFFICULTY, DYNAMICS, CHECKPOINTS, POWER-UPS, CAR TOUGHNESS, DEFORMATION, 3D COLLISIONS, DAMAGE, LANE ASSIST, TUTORIAL. It shows ONLY the rows a given mode needs (td5_raceopts_row_available) and PAGINATES (7/page, PREV/NEXT only when >7 rows). PLAYER NAME moved to its own OPTIONS-hub top row (Enter-to-edit inline). Confirm per mode: (1) open RACE OPTIONS in Single Race / Time Trial / Cop Chase / Drag / Cup / Quick Race and each MP mode (race/cup/traffic-battle/cop-chase/drag) and verify exactly the expected rows appear, cycle, and PREV/NEXT + a PAGE x/y footer show only when >7 rows; (2) checkpoints hidden in MP + cop chase + drag; power-ups hidden in drag; traffic hidden in drag; opponents hidden in drag/cup/traffic-battle; difficulty hidden at 0 opponents / quick race / cup / MP cop chase; lane-assist + tutorial single-player only; (3) OK/BACK/ESC persist to td5re.ini [GameOptions]; (4) SP drag -> car-select pass 2 OK opens RACE OPTIONS then OK launches, BACK re-enters the opponent car-select with cars preserved; MP drag shows it once (no loop); faithful cup shows it only before race 1; (5) OPTIONS hub PLAYER NAME row edits + persists the name; the old GAME OPTIONS row is gone and StartScreen=13 redirects to the hub. Netplay 2-instance: host sets POWER-UPS/COLLISIONS/TOUGHNESS/DAMAGE, client with opposite INI -> both peers match, no desync, client td5re.ini untouched.

summary: crash.log now includes GPU draw-ring forensics on a driver crash
detail: When the SEH crash handler fires (main.c), crash.log now gets an extra "GPU crash diagnostics" section: a live backend snapshot (device/context/swapchain pointers, device_generation, device_removed, present_count, current SRV, rt/target sizes, diag_context) plus the DRAW WATCH ring -- the last 256 draw submissions, each with its primitive/vertex/index counts AND the SRV + device_generation bound AT DRAW TIME. Any recent draw whose gen is BELOW the current device_generation is flagged "<-- STALE (pre-reset resource)" -- the smoking gun for the device-lost stale-bind crash class (nvwgf2um.dll null-deref, access=0). The ring is always-on (a few memory writes per draw, no disk I/O, no env var); it dumps only from the crash handler. Confirm: force a crash (e.g. TD5RE_FORCE_DEVICE_LOST on a build without the in-race self-heal, or any real crash) -> crash.log has the "GPU crash diagnostics" + "DRAW WATCH (SEH crash)" sections, and normal play is unaffected (no perf change, no extra files). This is diagnostics only -- separate from the dev-only D3D debug-layer InfoQueue drain (TD5RE_D3D_DEBUG), which stays out of this build.

summary: In-race HUD survives a GPU device reset (TDR) without crashing to results
detail: A GPU driver reset (TDR / DEVICE_HUNG) DURING a race left the in-race VectorUI shaders dangling and crashed the NVIDIA driver (nvwgf2um.dll null-deref, access=00000000) on subsequent frames -- often surfacing on the race->results transition (the recurring "transition/results crash"). Root cause: the analog gauge dial (s_ps_gauge/s_gauge_cb via td5_vui_gauge) and crisp HUD text (s_ps_msdf via td5_vui_msdf_quad) are frontend-module statics rebuilt only by frontend_ensure_vui_shaders(), which ran ONLY from td5_frontend_render_ui_rects (the frontend loop) -- never in-race. After a device-generation bump those cached shader pointers belonged to the freed device; the draw primitives guarded only against NULL, not a stale non-NULL pointer, so they bound a dead-device shader -> driver deref. Fix (td5_frontend.c): td5_vui_gauge and td5_vui_msdf_quad now call the idempotent frontend_ensure_vui_shaders() BEFORE their NULL guard, so the in-race HUD self-heals on a generation bump exactly like the frontend does (a failed rebuild falls back to the early-out / plain quad). Render-only; no sim/golden impact. Verify: race with TD5RE_FORCE_DEVICE_LOST=<mid-race frame> -> the device recovers and the race continues to results with NO crash.log (pre-fix this wrote crash.log with the nvwgf2um.dll stack and died). Also confirm the speedo/tacho dial and HUD text render correctly for the rest of the race after the forced reset (not blank).

summary: Split-screen FFB follows your own car after a screen swap
detail: Force feedback (crash/gear/landing/terrain/wall/prop/V2V/arrest jolts + steering/redline/drift rumble) in local split-screen was replayed from the wrong car after picking a non-default arrangement on the "CHOOSE YOUR SCREEN" position screen. Root cause: ff_local_actor_slot() (td5_input.c) resolved the actor via g_actorSlotForView[player] -- the pane->displayed-car map, which the position picker PERMUTES -- so player p's wheel/pad felt whatever car pane p happened to show, not the car player p drives (P1 rumbled from other players'/AI cars). The driven-car binding is identity (player p drives actor slot p; original @0x4288E0 has no view indirection), matching the button/camera path's view_for_player(). Fix branches ff_local_actor_slot(): NETPLAY -> td5_net_local_slot() (a client's own car is a non-zero slot, unchanged from the 2026-06-23 fix); LOCAL play (single + split-screen) -> identity (player). Device index (dev=player) and the per-actor physics-side FX arming are unchanged. FFB is output-only so golden traces are untouched. Confirm: 2-player split-screen race, pick a SWAPPED screen arrangement, crash the OTHER car into a wall -> P1's pad stays silent (only reacts to P1's own car). Regression check: netplay-loopback selftest stays green; on a net client the wheel still feels its own (non-zero-slot) car.

summary: RACE OPTIONS power-ups shows OFF / CASUAL / CHAOS
detail: The RACE OPTIONS screen (Screen_RaceOptions, reached from track-select in both single-player and MP split-screen setup) exposed power-ups as a 2-state OFF/ON toggle (td5_raceopts_value/td5_raceopts_cycle used on_off[powerups & 1] and powerups ^= 1), which hid the CHAOS tier even though the arcade backend (td5_arcade.c, g_td5.ini.powerups==2) and the Game Options screen (td5_gameopts, powerups_lv[]={OFF,CASUAL,CHAOS}) already implement all three states. Fixed RO_POWERUPS to mirror the 3-state model: value now reads powerups_lv[clamp(powerups,0,2)] and cycle steps 0->1->2->0. Confirm: open RACE OPTIONS from a split-screen MP race setup, scroll POWER-UPS -> it cycles OFF, CASUAL, CHAOS (not just OFF/ON); pick CHAOS and start the race -> a power-up box appears in every lane (Mashed-style chaos spawn).

summary: Shift-up / shift-down arrow indicators on the rev dial
detail: New PORT enhancement (td5_hud.c, speedometer block): a flashing UP triangle (green) draws just above the rev dial when engine revs reach the top of the range (default top 10%, i.e. revs >= 90% of redline) prompting a shift up; a flashing DOWN triangle (amber) draws when revs fall too low in a forward gear (default <= 25% of redline, gear 2nd/byte-3 or higher = lugging) prompting a shift down. Only one shows at a time, drawn per-pane so each split-screen player sees their own, blinking ~5.5 Hz off td5_plat_time_ms. Active in ALL race modes incl. drag. Gear byte 0=R 1=N 2=1st..7=6th; up shown only in a forward gear below top gear, down only in 2nd gear or higher. Knobs TD5RE_SHIFT_INDICATOR (1=on default,0=off), TD5RE_SHIFT_UP_PCT (default 90), TD5RE_SHIFT_DOWN_PCT (default 25). Confirm: drive with a manual gearbox (drag mode forces manual) -> green up-arrow flashes near the redline, amber down-arrow flashes when bogging the engine in a high gear; verify it appears in every pane of a split-screen race and does not show in neutral/reverse.

summary: Analog steering centre deadzone (no more phantom self-steer)
detail: New PORT fix (td5_input.c, analog Path B): a worn/off-centre analog stick reported a small non-zero X axis with hands off the stick, so the car steered to one side on its own -- reported in 5-player split-screen on player 1. Added a centre deadzone that zeros deflection within +/-DZ of centre, then LINEARLY RESCALES the remaining span back onto the full range so full lock is still reached at full deflection (no loss of steering range -- steering hard off a wall stays responsive). Applies to analog stick steering only; digital/keyboard is untouched. Stacks on top of any platform DirectInput deadzone. Knob TD5RE_STEER_DEADZONE (raw axis units out of ~250, default 20; 0 = faithful/off). Confirm with a controller (ideally the multi-pad 4-5 player split-screen setup that showed the drift): with hands off the stick the car tracks straight in every pane, and steering still reaches full lock and counter-steers off a wall without feeling sluggish. Tune with TD5RE_STEER_DEADZONE if 20 is too much or too little.

summary: Dynamic per-player traffic cap (each racer anchors up to 16, shared pool)
detail: Traffic used to have a single global on-road cap (max 16 = the whole pool). Now each racer (human OR AI) anchors its own traffic bubble and the effective cap = per-anchor volume budget (up to 16) times the number of spatially SEPARATE racer clusters, bounded by the traffic pool. Racers close together fall in one cluster and SHARE a single budget; a racer that pulls more than one bubble-radius away forms its own cluster and opens a fresh budget; once the pool is exhausted separated racers COMPETE for it (a far-ahead racer eats into the others' share). Implementation (td5_ai_traffic.c): trf_anchor_count() enumerates active racers (humans + AI opponents, contiguous low slots); trf_dyn_cluster_count() union-finds anchors within a share radius; trf_dyn_cap() = per*clusters clamped to TD5_MAX_TRAFFIC_SLOTS; min_player_dist + starved_player_span now scan ALL anchors so AI keep/refill their own bubbles; the ACTIVE retire test becomes union-of-bubbles (keep a car within front_keep of ANY anchor) when the feature is on. Traffic pool raised 16->64 (TD5_MAX_TRAFFIC_SLOTS) and the render/per-slot array bound TD5_ACTOR_MAX_TOTAL_SLOTS raised 32->80 in lockstep (static-asserted equal in td5_game.c) so slots 32-79 aren't out-of-bounds in the render loop. The 64 pool = ceiling of ~4 fully-separated 16-car bubbles; concurrent count is dynamic (16 x clusters, clamped to 64), NOT a fixed 64. Knobs: TD5RE_TRAFFIC_PERPLAYER_CAP=0 restores the single global cap; TD5RE_TRAFFIC_SHARE_SPANS=<n> overrides the share radius (default = despawn + scaled spawn-max). Verified: multi-AI Moscow race - clusters=1 bunched (eff_cap=16), and forcing separation gave clusters=2/3/4 -> eff_cap=32/48/64 (16 x clusters, clamped at 64); on_road reached 48 with no crash at up to 80 actors; structure lint clean. NOTE: this changes multi-racer traffic distribution so the golden Moscow/Pelton trace hashes will differ and need re-recording. PERF: watch frame time on a busy split-screen / full-grid race approaching 64 concurrent traffic (up to 80 total actors). Confirm: play with several AI and a split-screen partner - traffic is dense around every driver, thins only when the pool is spread thin across far-apart drivers.

summary: Traffic Battle keeps spawning traffic (wrecks auto-clear after 10s)
detail: In Traffic Battle, traffic volume is forced to Very-High so the concurrent traffic cap is already at the pool max (16). A wrecked traffic car was a PERMANENT wreck (TD5RE_WRECK_PERMANENT default ON -> ticks=-1) that kept its ACTIVE slot and kept counting toward the cap; it was only freed if the distance corridor happened to retire it. When wrecks pile up faster than the corridor clears them (heavy ramming, or a circuit where the corridor wraps) all 16 slots fill with permanent wrecks, on_road pins at the cap, and the dynamic spawner is gated off -> "traffic stops spawning partway through a battle". Fix (td5_ai_traffic.c): in battle mode a wrecked traffic car now ages a per-slot counter (s_trf_wreck_age) while ACTIVE+broken, and once it passes the despawn threshold it is faded out and its slot returned to the pool regardless of distance -- so fresh traffic keeps coming. Default 300 ticks (~10s @30Hz). Knobs: TD5RE_BATTLE_WRECK_DESPAWN=0 restores the permanent wreck; TD5RE_BATTLE_WRECK_DESPAWN_TICKS=<30..1800> tunes the delay. Scoped to Traffic Battle only (other modes keep permanent wrecks). Confirm: start a Traffic Battle, wreck a lot of oncoming cars near a spot / on a circuit -> wrecks visibly fade out ~10s after crashing and new traffic keeps spawning for the whole battle (previously the road went empty). Watch log/race.log for 'traffic_dyn_despawn ... wreck_expired=1'.

summary: Crash screen-shake gated so it doesn't rattle constantly in a crowded pack (UNVALIDATED)
detail: Bisecting a reported SUSTAINED split-screen camera wobble ("keeps going until the end of the race") pointed at the crash screen-shake (td5_camera_compute_crash_shake): td5_physics_get_crash_fx reports only the LATEST crash per slot, so in a 5-6 car pack continuous acute impacts keep resetting the shake's age to 0 and it never decays = a persistent rattle. Added a gate in td5_camera.c: (1) a camera-shake magnitude FLOOR (TD5RE_CRASH_SHAKE_MIN_MAG, default 500000 = 2x the physics acute threshold) so light pack bumps don't shake, only genuine hits; (2) a re-trigger COOLDOWN (TD5RE_CRASH_SHAKE_COOLDOWN, default 18 ticks) after a shake's full decay window before a NEW crash can re-arm, with the armed event cached so its decay is rendered from a stable copy immune to age-reset. Render-only. Whole gate reverts with TD5RE_CRASH_SHAKE_GATE=0. IMPORTANT - UNVALIDATED: this could NOT be confirmed headless because AI cars never crash acutely (0 crash-fx events even with traffic+cops), so the earlier "crash-shake is the wobble cause" bisect was a noise/frame-count artifact and is NOT proven. Needs a REAL crash-heavy 5-player repro to confirm. Confirm: run a crowded split-screen race with lots of contact -> compare default (gated) vs TD5RE_CRASH_SHAKE_GATE=0 (old, rattly) vs TD5RE_CRASH_SHAKE=0 (no shake at all). If the sustained wobble only goes away with CRASH_SHAKE=0 and the gate doesn't help, crash-shake magnitude/duration needs further tuning; if the gate already calms it, ship as-is. If NONE of them change it, the wobble is faithful pack physics (cars knocked around), not crash-shake.

summary: Chase camera no longer wobbles at the start of races after the first (split-screen)
detail: In multiplayer split-screen (5-6 panes) the chase camera could jolt/wobble at the START of the 2nd/3rd race in a session, on one or more panes, sometimes persisting. Root cause: td5_camera_set_preset (the per-race camera init from InitRace) only re-anchored the per-viewport smoothing/pose filters as a side effect of LoadCameraPresetForView, which runs ONLY for panes whose actor lookup succeeds (v < viewport_count, g_actor_table_base set, actor != NULL). Any pane that missed kept the PREVIOUS race's prev-pose, so the new race's first frame glided prev->cur across a teleport = start-of-race wobble, recurring per race per pane. The all-viewport fixer td5_camera_snap_smoothing() existed but was only wired to pause-resume, never race start; the recovery-glide arrays (s_recov_*) and wall-avoid clip ratios (g_camWallClipRatio/Apply) were never reset per race either. Fix (td5_camera.c): td5_camera_set_preset now UNCONDITIONALLY re-anchors smoothing + clears recovery-glide + resets wall-clip ratios to 1.0 for all 9 viewports at every race start. Render-only (camera never feeds the sim) so no physics/AI/replay/golden-trace impact. Knob TD5RE_CAM_RACE_SNAP=0 reverts. NOTE: this targets the START-of-race glide; a SUSTAINED wobble that keeps going through the race is a separate mechanism (FPS-coupled wall-avoid damping and/or crash-shake) not addressed here. Confirm: run several consecutive split-screen races (5-6 players, e.g. Jarash then Italy then Munich) -> no camera jolt at the start of the 2nd/3rd race on any pane.

summary: Device-lost recovery no longer crashes on the first frame back
detail: After a GPU TDR the recovery path (Backend_RecreateDevice + td5_plat_render_recover_textures) logged 'DEVICE RECOVERED ... rendering resumed' but the FIRST post-recovery frame NULL-derefed deep inside the NVIDIA driver (nvwgf2um.dll, crash.log access=00000000, whole stack in the driver). Root cause: recover_textures only refreshes the texture-page registry + main RT, so any other GPU-backed WrapperSurface/WrapperTexture (HUD/fade/frontend textures bound via Dev3_SetTexture) kept a dangling SRV from the freed device and handed it straight to PSSetShaderResources. Seen after a TDR while exiting the attract/demo race. Fix (ddraw_wrapper): Dev3_SetTexture now brings the parent surface device-current (WrapperSurface_EnsureDeviceCurrent, cheap generation early-out) before reading its SRV, rebuilding it from retained CPU pixels; EnsureDeviceCurrent also re-syncs the linked texture wrapper's cached SRV; Surface4_Release nulls the texture's back-pointer so the device-current guard can't deref a freed surface; and cached current_srv pointers (global + rec-ctx) are nulled on device release. Verified with TD5RE_FORCE_DEVICE_LOST=900 on an AutoRace: forced TDR at frame 903 recovered to generation=3/4 and the process kept rendering with NO crash.log (pre-fix this always wrote crash.log and died). Confirm: with TD5RE_FORCE_DEVICE_LOST=<frame> the game blips and keeps running; and a real TDR (e.g. finishing on Montego Bay) recovers without a crash instead of a black-screen/kill.

summary: View Replay cinematic cameras capped view distance (TDR guard)
detail: The trackside/orbit replay cameras view the track from a distance so the whole fwd/back span window is in frame at once (unlike the chase cam), which spiked a single draw past the GPU TDR watchdog ("draw ran too long" -> DEVICE_HUNG) during View Replay at high VIEW distances. td5_render_mesh.c now caps the effective view distance to 0.65 while a ghost replay is actually showing its trackside cameras (g_replay_mode && trackside_ready); live gameplay and the chase-fallback replay are untouched. Knob TD5RE_REPLAY_CAM_VIEW_DIST (default 0.65, clamp [0.15,1.0]; 1.0 disables). NOTE: separate from the recurring nvwgf2um.dll driver crash seen on transition/results screens. Confirm: View Replay on Moscow at ViewDistance=100 shows cinematic cameras without a GPU hang; distant scenery pops a little sooner during the replay only.

summary: View Replay ends at end of recording (no freeze on last pose)
detail: Ghost View Replay poses from a fixed buffer and had no natural stop -- past the last recorded frame it froze on the final pose forever, very visible for a recording cut short by END RACE NOW. It now fades out to results when playback reaches the end of the recording (same path as the ESC abort). Confirm: use END RACE NOW early, View Replay -> it plays the captured portion then returns to results on its own; ESC still exits early.

summary: Dev pause menu: END RACE NOW available in single-player
detail: Dev builds now show END RACE NOW in the pause menu in single-player (release keeps it local-split-screen-MP-only) so QA can force-finish and jump to View Replay without driving the whole race. Confirm in a dev build: SP pause menu has END RACE NOW and it force-finishes to results; a release build still hides it in SP.

summary: View Replay trackside cameras cut between shots again
detail: Ghost-state View Replay skips the sim (physics/track walker), so the actor track-position block at +0x080 (track_span_raw etc.) -- normally produced each tick by the walker -- was frozen at the countdown-end value because td5_replay.c only recorded/restored the pose, not that block. SelectTracksideCameraProfile (td5_camera.c) keys the cinematic trackside replay camera on actor+0x80, so it never switched view for the whole replay; a frozen shot whose look-at kept panning to follow the receding car also looked like the camera "moving around" at the start. Fix: TD5_ReplaySnap now records the +0x080 block (span raw/normalized/accumulated/high-water, contact vertices A/B, sub-lane) each tick and replay_pose_actor restores it, so the span advances during replay. Record path is read-only -> normal sim/goldens unaffected. Confirm: drive a race on a track with authored trackside cameras (e.g. Moscow, Newcastle), hit View Replay -> the camera CUTS between trackside shots as the car advances (not one static/panning shot), and there is no camera drift at the race start.

summary: Frame-rate cap when VSync off (prevents GPU-overload TDR)
detail: The results/frontend loop was presenting effectively unsynced at ~35000 FPS (log: game_state=1, fps~37000, fe_present~0ms), flooding the GPU with back-to-back submissions and no idle -- the submission storm that drove the race->results transition into a GPU DEVICE_HUNG (TDR / black screen). Added a frame-rate cap in td5_plat_present (td5_platform_win32_window.c): when the present is effectively unsynced (caller vsync=0 OR Display VSync off), it paces successive presents to a max FPS via td5_plat_time_us + a sleep(bulk)+spin(remainder) wait. No effect when VSync is on (vblank already paces). Knob TD5RE_FRAME_CAP=<fps>, default 300 (0 = uncapped, e.g. benchmarking). 300 is ~100x below the rate that hung the GPU and imperceptible for play/menus. This is the PREVENTION that pairs with the device-lost RECOVERY shipped the same day. Confirm: run with VSync OFF (Display options or INI VSync=0), sit on the results screen / a menu -> FPS overlay shows ~300 not tens of thousands, GPU load drops, and finishing a Montego Bay race no longer hangs. With VSync ON, FPS still follows refresh unchanged. TD5RE_FRAME_CAP=0 restores uncapped.

summary: GPU device-lost recovery (no more black screen on TDR)
detail: On some tracks (reported on Montego Bay) finishing a 1-lap race caused a GPU DEVICE_HUNG (a Windows TDR: a draw/dispatch ran too long, seen with the results screen running uncapped at ~35k FPS). The D3D11 wrapper already latched the removed device to avoid a driver NULL-deref crash (Backend_NoteDeviceRemoved -> g_backend.device_removed) but had NO recovery, so the screen went black permanently and the process had to be killed (log/gpu_device_lost.log). Added Backend_RecreateDevice() in the wrapper: on a latched device-lost, the present path (plat_try_recover_device in td5_platform_win32_window.c) releases all backend-owned GPU objects, recreates the D3D11 device + swap chain + render targets + shaders + state objects + buffers (shared Backend_InitDeviceResources so create/recreate can't drift), bumps a new g_backend.device_generation, then rebuilds every game texture from its retained CPU copy (sys_buffer) -- eagerly for the texture-page registry + main RT (td5_plat_render_recover_textures), lazily for any other surface via a generation stamp checked in WrapperSurface_FlushDirty/EnsureDeviceCurrent. Recovery is rate-limited (~every 30 frames) and capped (240 attempts) so a truly dead adapter doesn't spin forever. Test with TD5RE_FORCE_DEVICE_LOST=<frame> (dev builds) to simulate a TDR. Confirm: finish a race on Montego Bay and other tracks -> no permanent black screen; and with TD5RE_FORCE_DEVICE_LOST set, the game blips and keeps rendering with textures intact (check log for 'DEVICE RECOVERED').

summary: View Replay reproduces the driven race exactly (ghost-state)
detail: View Replay was a deterministic re-simulation (recorded only the two player-input DWORDs + the per-race RNG seed, then re-ran physics/AI/dynamic-traffic-spawn hoping for an identical result). It drifted: FP accumulation over thousands of ticks plus spawn-cadence coupling made opponents and traffic diverge ("not the race I drove"). Replaced with a full ghost-state recorder (new td5_replay.c): during the real race every actor's per-tick transform (world_pos, rotation_matrix, euler/display angles, speed/slip/gear/brake/wheel angles) is snapshotted for the player, all AI opponents AND traffic; on View Replay the sim (td5_ai_tick + race-end brake + td5_physics_tick + metrics + arcade) is skipped entirely and actors are posed straight from the buffer with velocities zeroed. Traffic is not spawned by logic -- its exact per-tick draw alpha is recorded and re-applied via td5_ai_traffic_replay_force, so which traffic cars are on-road (and their fade-in/out) reproduces precisely. Memory-only (no disk). Knob TD5RE_GHOST_REPLAY=0 reverts to the legacy input re-sim. Confirm: drive a race with traffic + AI opponents (e.g. Moscow and a circuit like Newcastle), hit View Replay -> every opponent and traffic car follows the same path/positions as the driven race, no drift; also confirm split-screen and a traffic-heavy track.

summary: Headlights stay off in daytime on Montego Bay / Tokyo
detail: The auto-headlight sky-darkness threshold (s_auto_sky_thr in td5_render_mesh.c) was 110, calibrated only on Moscow(63.5) vs a ~124 daytime reference. It wrongly flagged bright DAYTIME tracks whose average FORWSKY luma lands in the 95-117 band -- Montego Bay (95.6) and Tokyo (104.4) -- as "dark everywhere", so those cars ran headlights the whole race in broad daylight. Re-measured all 19 native FORWSKY averages: genuinely dark = Washington 35.6, Moscow 63.5; bright daylight starts at Montego 95.6. Lowered the threshold to 80 (midpoint of 63.5 and 95.6). Confirm: race Montego Bay and Tokyo in daytime -> car headlights OFF (no auto-glow), while Moscow and Washington still auto-light. Knob TD5RE_LIGHT_AUTO_SKY overrides.

summary: Uphill slope slow-down softened 65% (cars hold speed on climbs)
detail: The PORT-ONLY gravity-along-slope uphill deceleration term (td5_physics.c section 14a-slope, knob TD5RE_SLOPE_DECEL) had its default multiplier lowered from 2.0 to 0.70, i.e. the uphill decel "debuff" is now 65% less effective (0.70 = 35% of the old 2.0 strength). Downhill (accelerating) side is untouched (always x1). The gear-1-free crawl, top-speed light scale, and mass-relative weight scale all still compose on top. Restore the old feel with TD5RE_SLOPE_DECEL=2.0. NOTE: this changes the SIM, so the Moscow + TD6 Pelton golden traces must be re-recorded (TD5RE_TRACE_GOLDEN_UPDATE=1). Confirm on a hilly track (e.g. Courmayeur/Scotland) that climbing a grade bleeds off noticeably less speed than before, and that downhill behaviour is unchanged.

summary: Self-test render-golden layer catches visual regressions
detail: New dev/QA net in td5_selftest.c: the two golden races (race-golden-moscow, race-golden-pelton) now also read back the composited backbuffer at 4 fixed sim ticks (130/220/310/400) via Backend_RequestCapture/GetCapture, reduce each frame to a coarse 16x16 luma grid, and compare against td5mod/src/td5re/render_goldens.txt with L1 + per-cell distance thresholds (TD5RE_SELFTEST_RENDER_L1/_CELL). Golden races run the debug overlay off and use dynamic fast-forward (cruise at the suite FF through the countdown and between capture points, drop to 1x only in a short runway around each capture tick) so the captured frame is reproducible without paying full real-time for the whole race. A 'V' verdict row is added to selftest_report.{csv,md}. Re-record with TD5RE_TRACE_GOLDEN_UPDATE=1 (same run records both trace + render goldens). This is the class that catches the recent race-end wipe translucency + banner z-fight bugs. Confirm: full suite records goldens on the first update run, a clean rerun reports the 'V' rows PASS, and deliberately regressing a visual (e.g. force the wipe translucent) makes the matching 'V' row FAIL with a nonzero exit code.

summary: Game Options save on Back exit (TUTORIAL off sticks)
detail: Game Options previously only committed s_game_option_* into g_td5.ini when the OK button was pressed; leaving via Back/Escape silently discarded every toggle. Root-caused from a TUTDIAG log: toggling TUTORIAL off then backing out left tutorial_overlay=1, so the overlay still armed at race start. The commit is now shared (td5_gameopts_commit) and also runs on the Back path (frontend_central_return_back when leaving GAME_OPTIONS). Confirm: Game Options -> TUTORIAL -> OFF, leave with Back (NOT OK), start a race -> overlay does NOT show; toggle ON + Back -> it returns; also confirm other rows (Traffic, Cops, Damage, etc.) persist across Back and a relaunch.

summary: Breaking down no longer loses the race (force-recovery + ghost)
detail: With CAR DAMAGE on, a knocked-out human car no longer counts as done (the race waits instead of ending around you). A centred CAR BROKE DOWN / PRESS <R or SELECT> TO CONTINUE prompt shows; pressing recovery tows the car 30 spans back (TD5RE_BREAKDOWN_SPANS_BACK), fully repairs it (TD5RE_BREAKDOWN_REPAIR_PCT), glides the chase camera to the new spot (TD5RE_RECOVERY_CAM_MS), and grants a ~3s invulnerable translucent ghost that flickers before ending (TD5RE_GHOST_TICKS/_BLINK_TICKS/_ALPHA). Ordinary stuck (not broken) recovery is unchanged (in-place, partial). Net path routes the request through the merged control_bits so every peer recovers on the same lockstep round - NEEDS A LAN TEST to confirm no desync. Confirm: crash until broken down, prompt appears, press to continue -> 30 spans back + camera glide + blinking ghost + invulnerable during ghost; race does not end while you sit broken down.

summary: Montego Bay countdown camera fly-in restored
detail: Montego Bay (level 39) was the only track excluded from the race-start orbiting fly-in cinematic (k_flyin_blocked_levels safety net) because the camera dipped underground / through the water during the 3-2-1. That root cause (cold-spawn garbage linear_velocity_y amplified into the camera eye + car mesh) was fixed by the |vy| clamp, so Montego is un-blocked and now runs the normal fly-in. Confirm on a Montego race (single-player AND split-screen) that the countdown camera sweeps in like other tracks and the car/eye never sink below the ground or water.

summary: Race-end wipe (when NOT finishing 1st) is solid black, not see-through
detail: The directional race-end wipe (black bars closing in for any non-1st finish / quit-to-menu) rendered ~50% translucent so the race showed through it. Root cause: the bars used a textured OPAQUE_LINEAR draw with the color-keyed FADEWHT page, which tripped the foliage-AA classifier (Backend_IsFoliageAA: alpha-test on + has_alpha + blend off), and Backend_ApplyStateCache then OVERRODE the opaque blend with SRCALPHA/INVSRCALPHA. Fixed by drawing the bars via td5_plat_render_draw_tris_flat (BLEND_OPAQUE, alpha-test off, bypasses the state cache/classifier). Confirm by losing/quitting a race and watching the wipe fully cover the screen, single-player AND split-screen, with foliage AA on.

summary: Fences/foliage/scenery render both sides, not one-face / missing textures
detail: Native-banner geometry scan (td5_track_scan_banner_pages) is now OFF by default (TD5RE_BANNER_PAIR_CULL=1 to re-enable). It was detecting 'coincident reverse-wound panels' to one-sided-cull native overhead banners, but false-flagged double-sided fences/guardrails/foliage - and pages SHARED with scenery - so the cull removed their camera-facing side: fences appeared on one side only and scenery lost textures on every native track. TD6 tracks unaffected (keep the k_td6_banner_pages hand list). Trade-off: native TD5 overhead START/sign banners (Tokyo/Moscow) may z-fight/mirror again - confirm whether that's acceptable or needs the narrower per-page fix. Confirm on Scotland/Edinburgh + Moscow + Tokyo + a few others: fences render both sides, no missing-texture faces on scenery, and check the START banners.

summary: Police reliably start chasing when you speed past a cop
detail: The cop ACQUIRE window was widened (gap [0,15]->[-8,60] spans, TD5RE_COP_TRIGGER_LO/HI) and the acquire speed relaxed to 85% of the speeding threshold (TD5RE_COP_TRIGGER_SPEED_PCT). Diag showed 329/330 idle-cop rejects failed the old gap gate (cops sat 250-290 spans away). Confirm in a Cops-on race that driving fast past a cruising cop actually triggers a chase, on both human single-player and split-screen.

summary: Crashed cops recover and re-engage instead of staying wrecked
detail: A cop that takes a hard hit no longer becomes a PERMANENT wreck; it gets a self-clearing recovery timer (TD5RE_COP_WRECK_RECOVER, TD5RE_COP_WRECK_TICKS, default ~5s) like a racer, so it rejoins the chase. Regular traffic still permanently wrecks. Confirm a cop that rams you and crashes gets going again and resumes pursuit rather than parking for the race.

summary: Traffic changes lanes smoothly (no rapid flip-flop)
detail: Lane-change hysteresis added to the smart-traffic chooser (TD5RE_TRAFFIC_LANE_HYST, MARGIN, COOLDOWN): a car commits to a target lane and holds it until a clearly better lane appears + a cooldown lapses. Diag showed 398 lane-flips with cars alternating lanes every ~2 ticks. Confirm traffic no longer jerks side-to-side and the flow looks natural.

summary: Wedged/stuck traffic frees itself instead of blocking the road
detail: Universal unstick backstop (TD5RE_TRAFFIC_UNSTICK, default on): any non-cop traffic car with zero span progress for ~3s is reset in place (realigned, velocity cleared, progress reseeded) so it rejoins the flow. Diag showed 24/36 stalls were low-speed wedges that AntiFreeze/collision-escape never caught, with 3 cars stuck permanently. Confirm cars no longer sit frozen at track hotspots.

summary: End-of-race transition is opaque; race doesn't bleed through RESULTS
detail: At race end the RESULTS/menu screen no longer shows the fading race scene through it (the player car dissolving over the results table). Root cause was the D3D11 flip-model swapchain retaining stale race frames when the clear-skipping frontend took over; now a forced clear runs for the first frames after a screen entry. Confirm the race->RESULTS handoff is a clean opaque cut on single-player AND split-screen MP. NOTE: a separate race-START edge-smear at non-default window/DPI is still under investigation.

summary: START/FINISH banners + track signs render solid, not 50% see-through
detail: Native TD5 track type-2 texture pages (START/FINISH gantry banners, signs, pennants) previously rendered ~50% translucent; now solid, matching the original (BindRaceTexturePage type-2 = color-key opaque, z-write off). Confirm on Montego + Moscow start lines and other tracks' signage.

summary: PENDING TO TEST list is now the editable pending_to_test.csv (summary,detail,status)
detail: The checklist is loaded from a tracked CSV next to the source (td5mod/src/td5re/pending_to_test.csv), no longer from compiled arrays. Edit it manually to add/reword/annotate/retire items. Confirm: the PENDING TO TEST screen still populates, ENTER opens the detail modal with the right note, the F11 in-race overlay lists items, and SUPR on a row makes it vanish AND stay gone after relaunch (its status flips to 'tested' in the CSV; only 'pending' or blank rows show).

RACE OPTIONS screen: adds checkpoint timers/power-ups/toughness/deform
