High Scores: COLLISIONS + AIR TIME columns match race results
High Scores: celebrity names show in full (Michael Schumacher)
High Scores: default best times all read 5-10 min (unraced tracks)
High Scores: single-player CUPS no longer in the browse list
High Scores: split-screen MP records every player by profile name
Race-start camera: no more over-water swing at Montego Bay (both SP+MP)
Game Options: PLAYER NAME edits INLINE in place (no pop-up), full-size font
Game Options: DAMAGE toggle ON=car damage+bar, OFF=both off (label 'DAMAGE')
Game Options: PREV/OK/NEXT aligned under the option-row column (120/220/320)
Tutorial: TUTORIAL=ON now shows the overlay on keyboard too (was pad-only)
PENDING TO TEST: ENTER opens a details modal (no more mark-tested)
detail: Highlight any row on the PENDING TO TEST list and press ENTER (pad A / mouse click) -- a centred modal should appear showing the full item text plus a longer test-focus note. Press ENTER, B, or ESC again to close it and return to the list at the same page/scroll position; paging (PREV/NEXT) and SUPR/DELETE should be inert while the modal is open.

summary: PENDING TO TEST: crossing-out is gone; SUPR/DELETE still clears an item
detail: Rows no longer show a [ ]/[x] checkbox and ENTER no longer strikes a row through -- it only opens the details modal now. SUPR/DELETE on the highlighted row should still work exactly as before: it removes the row immediately and it stays gone (tombstoned) on the next launch.

summary: Arcade NITRO: glowing speed trail + 1.5x top speed while active
Arcade GHOST: blocks new + ends an active cop chase
Arcade INDESTRUCTIBLE: no self-damage/speed loss from traffic or racers (walls unaffected)
Arcade SHIELD and ROCKET power-ups removed
Arcade FREEZE: ram slows victim to 1/3, glows, recovers over 3s
Arcade MAGNET: only spawns in Traffic Battle mode
Arcade REPAIR: actually repairs the car; damage-gated
Arcade POWER-UPS option: OFF / CASUAL / CHAOS (one per lane)
Cup CHOOSE YOUR TEAM: roster box (top right) lists members by name
detail: On the CHOOSE YOUR TEAM cup screen, look for a box in the top-right listing each team and its member profile names; it should update live as you and the host assign players/AI to teams, and the AI-skill slider was compacted to make room for it.

summary: MP mode select: buttons no longer overlap HOST banner text
detail: On the multiplayer SELECT GAME MODE screen, confirm the mode-select buttons sit clear of the HOST banner and mode-vote ring text at the top -- check as the actual host and also mid-vote, when border rings are drawn around a mode.

summary: MP mode select: TIME TRIAL entry is gone, other modes unaffected
detail: On the multiplayer SELECT GAME MODE list, TIME TRIAL should no longer appear at all (removed because ghost pass-through and checkpoint start/finish don't work correctly in MP); confirm the remaining modes still line up with no gap where it used to sit.

summary: MP profile screen: NAME + COLOUR button labels now centred
detail: On the MP split-screen profile-select screen, check the NAME and COLOUR button labels sit centred on their buttons rather than left-biased. Dev harness: TD5RE_MP_SIMUL_PREVIEW_PHASE=0 + --StartScreen=20 boots straight to this screen without a full MP lobby.

summary: Street lamps (EXPERIMENTAL, StreetLights=1 to enable): pools under posts
detail: Off by default -- set [Lighting] StreetLights=1 and drive past lamp posts at night/dusk; each nearby lamp should cast a warm light pool underneath it. Marked EXPERIMENTAL pending a look-dev pass, so rough pool shape/falloff is expected feedback, not necessarily a bug.

summary: Shadows: no duplicated/offset echo copies (fence area, Moscow)
detail: Drive the fence area on Moscow in daylight with shadows on and look for the reported bug: shadows appearing as several offset duplicate copies (a ray-march dithering artifact) rather than one clean shadow. Compare with SunShadows=0 to confirm they're really gone.

summary: Reflections P3: wet road mirrors cars/buildings on rainy Moscow
detail: Race Moscow in rain with [Lighting] Reflections=1; the road should turn wet-looking and reflect nearby cars/buildings, ray-traced against the scene rather than a flat wet tint.

summary: Reflections P3: car paint/glass sheen at grazing angles
detail: With reflections on, watch your car's paint and windows while turning -- at grazing/shallow viewing angles they should show a mirror-like environment sheen, not a flat unlit surface.

summary: Reflections P3: no smearing/ghosting artifacts while driving
detail: Drive at speed with reflections enabled and watch reflective surfaces (wet road, paint/glass) for smearing or ghosting trails -- the reflection should track cleanly frame to frame.

summary: Reflections P3: Reflections=0 removes all of it (A/B)
detail: A/B test: set [Lighting] Reflections=0 and confirm every reflection effect above disappears completely, leaving flat surfaces -- the master kill-switch for the whole feature.

summary: Shadows P2: cars/walls cast shadows on the road (drive daylight)
detail: Drive any track in daylight and confirm your car and roadside walls/buildings cast a visible shadow onto the road as you pass -- the base shadow-casting pass.

summary: Shadows P2: no shadow acne/flicker on the road while driving
detail: While driving in daylight with shadows on, watch the road surface for shadow acne (speckled noise) or flicker frame-to-frame -- edges should stay clean and stable, not shimmer or crawl.

summary: Shadows P2: tunnels/ambient zones cast NO sun shadow
detail: Drive through a tunnel or a track's ambient/shaded zone and confirm NO directional sun shadow is drawn inside it -- those zones use ambient lighting only.

summary: Shadows P2: headlight beam blocked by the car in front
detail: At night with headlights on, tail another car closely and check that its body blocks your headlight beam -- you should see a shadow cast by the car ahead onto the road/car behind it.

summary: Shadows P2: SunShadows=0 removes all of it (A/B)
detail: A/B test: set [Lighting] SunShadows=0 and confirm every sun-shadow effect above disappears -- the master kill-switch for the shadow-casting pass.

summary: UI GUIDE (changelog screen): widgets render + modal works
Retired screens: StartScreen=3/4 lands on main menu
InputScript: scripted drive (gas/brake/horn/keys) works
StartScreen: jumped-to menus look same as navigated
Lighting v2: authored zone colours show (dusk/tunnel tint)
detail: Drive through a track's authored lighting zones (dusk section, tunnel) and confirm the intended colour tint actually shows, rather than looking uniformly lit everywhere.

summary: Lighting v2: headlights don't shine through walls (N.L)
detail: At night with headlights on, put a wall/building between the headlight and a surface behind it -- the beam should NOT shine through; lighting should respect the surface normal (N.L) and be occluded by geometry.

summary: Lighting v2: Mode=0 looks identical to the old build (A/B)
detail: A/B test: set [Lighting] Mode=0 and confirm the scene looks the same as before the lighting v2 rework -- this mode is a faithfulness fallback, so any visible difference here is a regression.

summary: PLAYER NAME option: results row + high-score prefill
detail: Set your player name in Game Options, finish a race, and confirm your typed name (not 'PLAYER 1') appears in the results row; also check the high-score name-entry screen prefills with the same name instead of starting blank.

summary: Damage bar: top-centre + pause-style blue-red fill
detail: In a race with car damage on, confirm the health bar sits top-centre and fills/drains using the same blue-to-red gradient style as the pause menu's bars, not a plain solid colour.

summary: Damage bar on: checkpoint timer sits below it
detail: With the damage bar visible and checkpoint timers enabled, confirm the checkpoint timer is positioned below the damage bar rather than overlapping or sitting above it.

summary: Police pullover: brakes hold all the way through (no coast gap)
detail: Get pulled over by a cop and watch the deceleration -- braking should be continuous all the way to a stop, with no gap where the car coasts freely partway through the sequence.

summary: Pause menu: END RACE NOW only in local split-screen MP (not SP/net)
detail: Open the pause menu in single-player and in a networked race and confirm END RACE NOW is absent in both; open it in local split-screen MP and confirm it IS present there.

summary: Pause menu: RESTART/QUIT/EXIT/LOBBY ask YES/NO confirm in split-screen MP
detail: In local split-screen MP, open the pause menu and pick RESTART, QUIT, EXIT, or LOBBY -- each should raise a YES/NO prompt before acting, not execute immediately.

summary: Pause menu (single-player): RESTART/QUIT/EXIT act immediately, no confirm
detail: In single-player, open the pause menu and pick RESTART, QUIT, or EXIT -- these should act immediately with no YES/NO prompt, unlike split-screen MP.

summary: END RACE NOW: next race camera starts normal (was stuck shifted)
detail: Use END RACE NOW to force-finish a race, then start the next race and check the chase camera begins in its normal default position, not stuck shifted from the forced finish.

summary: Track walker: no span warp on degenerate quads (race normally)
detail: Race normally on any track and watch for a sudden position 'warp'/teleport as the car crosses a span boundary -- degenerate (malformed) track quads used to snap the walker incorrectly.

summary: Headlights: per-pixel beams flood the road (deferred light pass)
detail: At night with headlights on, confirm the beam is a proper per-pixel lit cone flooding the road (via the deferred lighting pass), correctly lighting bumps/curves, not a flat decal.

summary: Headlights auto-on in rain/overcast/dusk, off in bright daylight
detail: Drive across different weather/time conditions without touching the headlight control -- they should switch on automatically in rain/overcast/dusk and stay off in bright daylight.

summary: Headlights: verify on a sunny track they stay OFF (Maui/Sydney)
detail: Race Maui or Sydney (bright, sunny reference tracks) and confirm headlights stay OFF the whole race -- the negative-case check for the auto-headlight logic above.

summary: Split cup: later-race player can always accelerate (was camera-only)
detail: In a split-screen cup with multiple races, check a non-lead player's car in race 2+ can accelerate normally -- a bug had only the camera advancing correctly while the car itself stayed throttle-limited.

summary: Pause menu: END RACE NOW force-finishes (YES/NO confirm; any player)
detail: Open the pause menu as ANY player (not just host) in split-screen and pick END RACE NOW; confirm it raises a YES/NO prompt and, on YES, force-finishes for everyone.

summary: END RACE NOW: places ranked by current track progress, no DNFs
detail: After using END RACE NOW, check results rank every player by how far along the track they'd actually gotten -- nobody should show as DNF; everyone gets a placed result.

summary: END RACE NOW: net race ends + shows results on all machines together
detail: In a networked race, have one player trigger END RACE NOW and confirm the race ends and the results screen appears at roughly the same time on every connected machine.

summary: Alt+Enter fullscreen uses the display's native refresh, not 60Hz
detail: Press Alt+Enter to toggle fullscreen during gameplay and check the observed refresh rate matches your monitor's native refresh (e.g. 120/144Hz), not capped at 60Hz.

summary: Paint: SELECT CAR PAINT panel - secondary colour + pattern + 32 presets
detail: Open SELECT CAR PAINT and confirm it offers a secondary colour picker, a pattern selector, and roughly 32 preset combinations, not just the single primary-colour picker from before.

summary: Paint: Two-Tone/Stripes/Split show in BOTH menu preview and in-race body
detail: Pick a Two-Tone, Stripes, or Split pattern in the paint panel and confirm it renders on both the car-select preview AND the actual car body once in a race -- one-place-only is a bug.

summary: Lane Assist at a fork: picks one branch a few spans early, not the divider
detail: Turn Lane Assist on and approach a fork -- it should commit to one branch a few spans BEFORE the fork rather than aiming at the physical divider/wall between the branches.

summary: Split: one pad seen as 2 gamepads no longer joins as 2 players (cam bleed)
detail: If you have a controller Windows enumerates as two devices, confirm the split-screen lobby only lets it join as ONE player -- it could previously join as two, causing camera/input bleed between panes.

summary: Reset car (split-screen): resets in place, no teleport-to-start/wall
detail: In split-screen, press reset-car on a stuck/wedged car and confirm it resets IN PLACE (same spot, corrected orientation), not teleporting to the track start or into a wall.

summary: Reset car: fully repairs (health+dents); un-sticks a knocked-out car
detail: With damage on, wreck/knock out a car, then reset-car and confirm it comes back with full health AND cleared dents -- specifically test un-sticking a fully knocked-out car, not just a lightly damaged one.

summary: Tutorial overlay now shows at the START of every race (was once-ever)
detail: Start several races in a row (gamepad connected) and confirm the controller tutorial overlay appears at the start of EACH one, not just the very first race ever played.

summary: Game Options: new TUTORIAL on/off row turns the overlay off
detail: Open Game Options, find the TUTORIAL row, toggle it OFF, then start a race and confirm the tutorial overlay no longer appears (toggle back ON to confirm it returns).

summary: Game Options paginated (2 pages, < PREV / NEXT >); all rows fit, values/arrows OK
detail: Open Game Options and confirm it's split across 2 pages with < PREV / NEXT >, every row is fully visible with nothing clipped, and each row's value + selector arrows still work after paging.

summary: Car defaults to your PROFILE colour (TD6 exact / TD5 nearest); PAINT overrides
detail: Set an accent colour on your profile, pick a car without touching PAINT -- it should default to your profile colour (exact match for TD6, nearest available for TD5). Then set PAINT and confirm it overrides the profile default.

summary: MP profile: typed-but-unsaved NEW name auto-saves when the race starts
detail: On the MP profile screen, type a new profile name but do NOT explicitly save, then start the race -- the typed name should auto-save as a real profile rather than being discarded.

summary: Celebrity leaderboard: unraced tracks show celeb names (not Frank/Ben)
detail: Look at the leaderboard for a track you have never raced -- default names should be celebrity-style (e.g. Michael, Lewis, Ayrton) rather than the old generic Frank/Ben. Detection is exact-match against the original default names, so a track with a real saved score should be unaffected.

summary: Celebrity leaderboard: CelebrityNamesAPI=1 → fetches from randomuser.me
detail: Set CelebrityNamesAPI=1 (needs internet) and confirm celebrity names are fetched live from randomuser.me instead of the built-in static table; check the log for a successful fetch and that names apply and flush to the save immediately after load.

summary: Pause menu RADIO slider (row 2) sets radio volume live
detail: Open the pause menu during a race with radio playing, find the RADIO slider on row 2, and move it -- volume should change live, not just on menu close.

summary: Radio: quiet by default + mutes when minimized/paused/in menus
detail: Confirm the radio starts quiet by default and mutes automatically when the window is minimized, the game is paused, or you're back in frontend menus.

summary: Radio: live station plays during a race (needs internet)
detail: With internet available and radio enabled, start a race and confirm a live internet radio station actually plays audio during the race -- needs a real network connection to verify.

summary: Music seam wired: no audio regression (jukebox/race/pause)
detail: Move through jukebox/menu music, into a race, and into the pause menu, listening for any regression -- no missing tracks, no jarring cutoffs, no failure to resume across states.

summary: Traffic: visible ~60% further on open tracks (no short-range pop-out)
detail: On an open (non-TD6) track, watch traffic ahead as you approach at speed -- it should become visible noticeably further away (~60% further) rather than popping in at short range. Knob: TD5RE_TRAFFIC_VIEW_DIST (default ~1.6x); TD6 tracks are perf-capped separately, so test on a faithful TD5 track like Moscow.

summary: Damage Bar VISIBLE for every split-screen player (was invisible before)
detail: In split-screen with car damage on, confirm EVERY player's pane shows their own damage bar -- some panes could previously have an invisible/missing bar.

summary: Damage Bar OFF (Game Options): no top bar, no wreck-out; dents stay
detail: In Game Options turn the damage bar OFF, then race and take damage -- the top bar should be hidden and the car should never wreck/knock out from zero health, but dents should still appear normally.

summary: Damage Bar toggle is global: every split-screen player gets it or none
detail: In split-screen, toggle the damage bar and confirm it applies to ALL players' panes at once -- it's a single global setting, not per-player.

summary: Cops: accel rubber-band out-drags a faster car on straights (catch up)
detail: Get chased by a cop while driving a notably faster car and watch a long straight -- the cop should accelerate hard enough to close the gap despite lower top speed (an acceleration rubber-band, not a top-speed cheat). Knob: TD5RE_COP_CHASE_ACCEL (default 280). Headless verification needs TD5RE_COP_CHASE_AI=1 + PlayerIsAI=1 + AutoThrottle=0 since cops normally only chase HUMAN slots.

summary: Cops: tougher (300%); chasing cop shrugs off wall scrapes (no chase-end)
detail: Deliberately clip a chasing cop into a wall -- with durability boosted 300%, it should shrug the scrape off and keep chasing rather than the chase ending from a minor bump.

summary: Cops: ease off for corners instead of flooring into the outside wall
detail: Watch a chasing cop through a sharp corner -- it should visibly ease off the throttle for the corner rather than flooring it into the outside wall. Knob: TD5RE_COP_CORNER_EASE.

summary: Cops: pass a cluster and ALL of them chase you (no 1-cop limit)
detail: Drive past a cluster of multiple cop cars and confirm ALL of them start chasing, not just one -- the original one-chaser-per-target cap was removed.

summary: Cops: longer chase leash (110 spans) + 200% catch-up; harder to shake
detail: Get chased, then try to lose the cop by pulling ahead -- it should take noticeably longer/further to shake it: the chase persists up to a 110-span gap and catch-up is boosted 200%, so the cop keeps closing rather than giving up quickly.

summary: Traffic: stuck car no longer fades in front of you (gates on render dist)
detail: Watch traffic ahead for a car that gets stuck -- it should NOT despawn/fade while still within your render distance in front of you; the despawn gate now respects the render-cull distance (front_keep) instead of a fixed shorter range.

summary: Drag MP mode: SELECT GAME MODE -> DRAG RACE (no track pick, no AI)
detail: In multiplayer, open SELECT GAME MODE and pick DRAG RACE -- confirm it skips the normal track-selection screen entirely and adds no AI opponents; drag race MP is humans-only.

summary: Drag MP options: TRAFFIC on/off, DISTANCE, EXTRA LANES
detail: On the drag MP options screen, toggle TRAFFIC, pick a DISTANCE preset, and set EXTRA LANES, confirming each choice actually changes the race you get.

summary: Drag LONG/EPIC: strip+stadium truly extend, finish moves down it
detail: Pick LONG or EPIC and confirm the strip and stadium geometry are PHYSICALLY extended (not just a further finish marker on the original strip) -- you drive further over newly-inserted road/stadium tiles before the finish.

summary: Drag race: strip widens to one lane per car (field-size lanes)
detail: Start drag races with different player counts and confirm the strip width scales to one lane per car -- a 2-car race should be narrower than a 6-car race.

summary: Drag race: tap left/right to CHANGE LANES (NFS-Underground feel)
detail: During a drag race, tap left/right and confirm your car snaps into the adjacent lane with a quick, arcade lane-change feel, not a slow drift or no response.

summary: Drag race: longer race via TD5RE_DRAG_LENGTH (existing road reused)
detail: Set TD5RE_DRAG_LENGTH higher and confirm the drag race is proportionally longer, reusing existing road geometry rather than needing new assets.

summary: Lane Assist: road-centre aid, firmer further off; avoids grass
detail: Turn Lane Assist on, drift toward the road edge, and feel the correction -- gentle near centre, firmer further off-centre, always aiming for the drivable road (never the grass/slow lane) even at a fork. Baked constants: STRENGTH=900, MAX_YAW=400; feel previously user-confirmed 'perfect'.

summary: Lane Assist toggle: SP Game Options / MP Profile screen / L key
detail: Confirm Lane Assist can be turned on/off from THREE places -- single-player Game Options, the MP Profile screen (next to AUTO/MANUAL), and the L key live in a race -- and all three reflect the same setting.

summary: Traffic Battle: MP split-screen mode (below CUP) + solo (TD5RE_BATTLE=1)
detail: In multiplayer split-screen, find TRAFFIC BATTLE below CUP on mode-select; separately set TD5RE_BATTLE=1 and confirm you can also play it solo.

summary: Traffic Battle: WIN option MOST WRECKS or CHECKPOINTS (catch-up deadline)
detail: In Traffic Battle options, choose the win condition -- MOST WRECKS or CHECKPOINTS (a catch-up deadline) -- and test both, confirming the results/winner logic matches the chosen mode.

summary: Traffic Battle: oncoming traffic, no rivals/cops, spawn mid-track
detail: Start a Traffic Battle race and confirm traffic spawns oncoming (toward you), there are no AI rivals or cops, and players spawn mid-track rather than at a normal start line.

summary: Traffic Battle: crashes wreck at ANY angle/speed; airborne cars wreck too
detail: Hit a traffic car at various angles and speeds (including glancing hits) and confirm each registers as a wreck; also confirm a car airborne on impact still wrecks correctly.

summary: Traffic Battle: wrecked cars go translucent + pass-through (drive thru)
detail: After wrecking a traffic car, confirm its wreck goes translucent and you can drive straight through it, rather than it staying solid and blocking the lane.

summary: Traffic Battle: live WRECKS HUD; results sort/label by WRECKS
detail: During Traffic Battle confirm a live WRECKS counter is on the HUD; at the end confirm results sort/label by WRECKS rather than the normal race-position columns.

summary: Arcade: new power-ups SHIELD/EMP-FREEZE/MAGNET/ROCKET/REPAIR (HAZARD off)
detail: Play an Arcade race and grab boxes until you've seen SHIELD, EMP-FREEZE, MAGNET, ROCKET, and REPAIR; confirm each does something distinct and the old HAZARD (oil) no longer appears.

summary: Game Options: CAR TOUGHNESS + DEFORMATION levels (Low/Norm/High)
detail: Open Game Options, set CAR TOUGHNESS and DEFORMATION to different Low/Normal/High combinations, and confirm crashes feel/look different accordingly.

summary: Car damage ON all races: dents+scuff, smoke, wreck, finish orbit cam
detail: Race with damage on and confirm the full chain: dents/scuff accumulate, smoke appears on a badly damaged car, the car can wreck/knock out, and finishing triggers the orbit finish cam.

summary: Chase cam: no vertical jitter at race start on high-refresh (120/144+)
detail: On a high-refresh display, watch the chase camera closely in the first second or two of a race start -- it should be smooth with no vertical jitter/bounce. Fallback if it persists on a given rig is a terrain-tilt IIR filter, not fully confirmed on every setup.

summary: MP car select: host X/TAB menu sets everyone's car (same/slow/avg/fast)
detail: As host on MP car-select, open the X/TAB quick menu and pick 'same car', 'slowest', 'average', or 'fastest' -- confirm it reassigns EVERY player's/AI's car, not just your own.

summary: MP cup: player 1 keeps chosen car on race 2+ (no pre-cup car revert)
detail: Start an MP cup, have player 1 pick a specific car, complete race 1, and check race 2 onward -- player 1 should keep the chosen car rather than reverting to a pre-cup car.

summary: ARCADE: power-up timer bar shows the full duration (not last secs)
detail: Grab a timed power-up in Arcade and watch its HUD timer from the moment you grab it -- it should show the FULL duration counting down, not only appear in the last few seconds.

summary: ARCADE: item boxes spawn one lane closer to track centre
detail: In Arcade, compare item box placement to the road edges -- boxes should sit one lane closer to centre than the very outer edge, making them easier to reach without hugging the wall.

summary: ARCADE: power-up effects last 20% longer (NITRO/GHOST/WRECK)
detail: Grab NITRO, GHOST, and WRECK in Arcade and time each effect -- each should run about 20% longer than the previous baseline duration.

summary: Power-ups now ARCADE-only: SIMULATION shows no item boxes
detail: Start a race with Dynamics set to SIMULATION and confirm NO item boxes spawn anywhere -- power-ups are strictly Arcade-only. Watch for a dynamics-commit timing edge case where a late mode switch could still let boxes through.

summary: MP splitscreen: gold HOST tag on profile/screen/mode/car selectors
detail: As session host in split-screen MP, check the profile, position, mode-vote, and car-select screens -- your selector should show a gold HOST pill badge on all four, not just some.

summary: Split car-select 5+ players: big car + buttons|stats two columns; 7-9p car stays big
detail: Join a split-screen car-select with 5+ players -- each narrow pane should show a big car preview on top with buttons/stats in two columns below, and the preview should stay reasonably large even at 7-9 players rather than shrinking to a thumbnail.

summary: Tutorial overlay: Xbox pad diagram (gamepad only); ALL players press to start; every race
detail: Start a race with a gamepad connected -- the overlay should show a real Xbox pad diagram with arrows from each control to its action; in split-screen, the race shouldn't start until ALL players press a button, and this should happen every race, not just the first.

summary: Tutorial overlay: TutorialOverlay=2 forces (bypass gamepad gate); =0 off; never in MP/replay/trace
detail: Set TutorialOverlay=2 and confirm the overlay shows even on keyboard-only (bypassing the gamepad-only gate); set =0 and confirm it never shows. Confirm it also never appears during networked MP, replay playback, or a trace/benchmark run regardless of the setting.

summary: MP cop chase: suspects can now pick TD6 cars, not just TD5
detail: In an MP cop chase lobby, as a suspect, open car select and confirm TD6 cars are selectable alongside TD5 -- suspects were previously restricted to TD5-only.

summary: Reverse direction now works on the 7 lap circuits (Newcastle etc)
detail: Pick a 7-lap circuit (e.g. Newcastle) with Reverse enabled and confirm the race runs correctly for the full lap count -- checkpoints, finish detection, and AI routing all working, not just the first lap or two.

summary: Controller: Y changes camera; horn moved to L3 (stick click)
detail: On a gamepad, press Y and confirm it cycles the camera view; click the left stick (L3) and confirm it sounds the horn -- horn was moved off its old binding onto L3.

summary: Time trial on city tracks (HK/London): mid-track start no insta-end
detail: Run a Time Trial on a TD6 city track (Hong Kong or London) that starts mid-track via a P2P checkpoint layout, and confirm the run does NOT instantly end right after starting.

summary: Replay no longer counts as a race (MP cup keeps real results)
detail: Play back a replay and confirm it isn't counted as an actual race; specifically, watching a replay between MP cup races should NOT affect or overwrite the real cup standings.

summary: MP cop chase: bust arrow shows only when you crash a suspect
detail: As a cop, ram a suspect and confirm a bust arrow/indicator appears tied to that crash event -- it should not show from mere proximity without a crash.

summary: MP cop chase: other players' name labels hidden
detail: In MP cop chase, look at other players' cars and confirm their floating name labels are hidden -- this mode intentionally suppresses labels, unlike normal MP racing.

summary: MP cop chase: no 1st/2nd; top shows per-cop ARRESTS scoreboard
detail: Check the top HUD during MP cop chase -- it should show a per-cop ARRESTS scoreboard, not a normal 1st/2nd/3rd race-position indicator.

summary: MP cop chase: arrest needs SIREN ON; else 'turn on siren' msg
detail: As a cop, try to arrest a suspect with siren OFF -- it should NOT count, with a 'turn on siren' message; turn the siren on and confirm the same crash now registers as a valid arrest.

summary: MP cop chase: HORN toggles each cop's own siren
detail: As a cop, press HORN and confirm it toggles YOUR OWN siren (light + sound) independently of any other cop's siren state in the same race.

summary: MP mode vote: A=vote -> profile-colour border ring on mode
detail: During an MP mode vote, press A on a mode option and confirm a border ring appears around it in YOUR profile's accent colour, marking your vote.

summary: MP mode vote: more voters = more nested rings; host decides
detail: Have multiple players vote for the same mode and confirm each voter adds another nested ring (their own colour) rather than just one ring total; confirm the HOST ultimately picks the winning mode.

summary: MP mode vote: arrow disappears once a player casts their vote
detail: Before voting a player's pane shows a selection arrow; once they vote (press A), confirm that arrow disappears for them (their vote now shows via the coloured ring instead).

summary: Time Trial after a cup: no instant end / right car on Race Again / no rivals on your lane
detail: Finish an MP cup, then start a Time Trial (or Race Again) -- confirm the run doesn't instantly end, you're driving the expected car (not a leftover cup car), and no AI rivals share your lane.

summary: Snow tracks (Bern) easier: ice surface grips more, less slide
detail: Race Bern and compare handling on the icy surface -- grip should be noticeably higher and the car should slide less than the original unmodified ice physics.

summary: Snow grip boost OFF on dry tracks (tarmac feel unchanged)
detail: Race a normal dry/tarmac track and confirm handling feels exactly as before the snow-grip change -- the ice-grip boost should apply ONLY to snow/ice surfaces.

summary: MP CUP podium: humans show profile colour, CPU shown in grey
detail: Finish an MP cup and check the podium -- human players show their own profile accent colour, CPU/AI opponents show grey, so humans are easy to tell from bots at a glance.

summary: Cops re-chase after you crash (was: 1 chase then never, esp. MP)
detail: Get chased, deliberately crash, recover, and keep driving -- a cop should be willing to chase you again; previously a cop would chase once and never re-engage for the rest of the race, especially in MP.

summary: Split MP: reset car resets YOUR car, not the other player
detail: In split-screen, have player 2 press reset-car and confirm ONLY player 2's car resets -- player 1 is completely unaffected.

summary: Split MP: change-camera affects YOUR pane, not the other
detail: In split-screen, have one player change camera view and confirm only THEIR pane's camera changes -- other panes keep their own independent camera.

summary: Split MP: rear-view affects YOUR pane (positioned panes)
detail: In split-screen (including non-default 'positioned' pane layouts), hold rear-view as one player and confirm only their pane shows it, regardless of pane arrangement.

summary: MP profile DELETE asks 'DELETE <name>? A=YES B=NO' first
detail: On the MP profile screen, select DELETE on a profile and confirm a prompt names that exact profile ('DELETE <name>?') with A=YES / B=NO, rather than deleting on the first press.

summary: Profile delete removes ONLY the named/selected profile
detail: Create a few profiles, delete one via the confirm prompt, and confirm ONLY that profile is removed -- all others (and anyone holding one) are unaffected.

summary: MP setup: ARCADE/SIM selector only on track select (not mode opts)
detail: Go through MP setup and confirm the ARCADE/SIMULATION selector appears on track-select and does NOT also duplicate on the mode-options screen.

summary: ARCADE: ~2x as many item boxes spawn along the track
detail: Compare item box density along the track in Arcade mode -- roughly twice as many boxes should spawn along the route, so you should rarely go long without seeing one.

summary: ARCADE: power-up effects last 2x longer (GHOST/WRECK/oil)
detail: Grab GHOST, WRECK, or the oil HAZARD in Arcade and time the duration -- each should last roughly double the original baseline (an earlier, larger duration pass than the later 20%-longer tweak).

summary: ARCADE NITRO: sustained 2.5x acceleration boost (~5s), not instant
detail: Grab NITRO and watch acceleration -- it should ramp into a sustained ~2.5x boost lasting about 5 seconds, not an instant one-shot speed jump that fades immediately.

summary: Collisions hug car silhouette (hull) - tight contact, no gap
detail: Clip the side of another car or wall at a shallow angle -- collision should trigger right at your car's visual silhouette/convex hull, no visible-but-no-collision gap.

summary: AI un-wedges without driving off-track (steers to interior)
detail: Watch an AI car that gets wedged against a wall or another car -- its recovery steering should aim toward the track interior, never driving off-track while un-wedging.

summary: AI steers away when wedged vs car/player (no infinite grind)
detail: Deliberately wedge an AI car against your own car and watch how long it stays stuck -- it should actively steer away to break the wedge, not grind indefinitely with no progress.

summary: ARCADE airborne launch dialled way down (was too high/unplayable)
detail: Hit a jump/ramp in Arcade and compare the airborne launch height/distance -- should be noticeably tamer than an earlier version that launched cars unplayably high.

summary: ARCADE power-ups now floating boxes you drive through
detail: Confirm item boxes in Arcade are floating cubes you simply drive INTO to collect, not a target you shoot or press a button on.

summary: Item box: glowing spinning pulsing cube w/ kind icon
detail: Look closely at an item box before grabbing it -- it should be a glowing cube that's both spinning and pulsing, with a small icon indicating which power-up kind it holds.

summary: Grabbed item box vanishes, respawns after 5 seconds
detail: Grab an item box, note its position -- it should vanish immediately then reappear in roughly the same spot about 5 seconds later, not stay gone or respawn instantly.

summary: One power-up at a time; can't grab another until it ends
detail: While holding a power-up, drive over another item box and confirm you can NOT pick up a second one until the current effect ends.

summary: Box frequency scales with human player count (100-300)
detail: Compare item box spawn frequency between a 1-human race and one with several humans -- more humans should mean noticeably more boxes (roughly a 100-300% scale), not a fixed rate.

summary: 5+ players: chance of paired boxes (left + right shoulder)
detail: With 5+ players, watch for item boxes spawning in PAIRS -- one on each shoulder of the road at roughly the same point -- as an extra spawn variant that starts at this player count.

summary: HAZARD oil (3 lanes): hit it = drift uncontrollably ~2.5s
detail: Hit a HAZARD oil slick (should span roughly 3 lanes) and confirm it causes an uncontrollable drift for about 2.5 seconds before you regain full control.

summary: Game Options: POWER-UPS on/off toggle (persists)
detail: In Game Options toggle POWER-UPS off, race and confirm no boxes spawn; restart the game and confirm the OFF state persisted rather than resetting to default.

summary: Item boxes start after span 100, sit low near the road
detail: Note where the first item box appears -- should not spawn before roughly span 100, and boxes generally sit low, near road height, not floating high above it.

summary: Item boxes sit on road edges; steer over to grab one
detail: Confirm item boxes sit along the road EDGES (not the centre), so you must steer toward one side to collect them rather than driving straight through the middle.

summary: Car SILHOUETTE glows the effect colour (not a blob)
detail: Grab a power-up with a visual effect (e.g. GHOST) and look at your car -- the glow should trace your car's actual silhouette/shape, not appear as a generic blob detached from it.

summary: GHOST: car turns translucent, passes through traffic
detail: Grab GHOST and drive into a traffic car or wall -- your car should turn visibly translucent and pass through it without colliding, for the effect's duration.

summary: Effect name shows centred below the checkpoint timer
detail: Grab any power-up and check the HUD -- the active effect's name should display centred, positioned below the checkpoint timer, not overlapping it or off to a side.

summary: Quick Race PHYSICS row toggles ARCADE/SIMULATION + fits OK/Back
detail: Open Quick Race and find the PHYSICS row -- toggling ARCADE/SIMULATION should actually change the race's dynamics, and the row should fit on screen alongside OK/BACK with no overlap.

summary: Minimap: on a branch both fork tracks render (not just dots)
detail: Drive up to a fork with the minimap visible -- both branches should render as actual road geometry, not just a couple of disconnected dots marking the branch point.

summary: MORE STATS: POWER captioned (engine output); WEIGHT under GRIP
detail: Open MORE STATS for a car and confirm POWER has a caption clarifying it's engine output, and WEIGHT sits under GRIP in the layout, not its old position.

summary: MORE STATS BALANCE shows a visual front/rear weight split bar
detail: In MORE STATS, confirm BALANCE is a visual bar showing the front/rear weight split (a marker on the bar), not just a plain number.

summary: Quick stat bars (under PAINT) match the MORE STATS scale
detail: Compare the quick stat bars under PAINT against the same car's bars in MORE STATS -- both should use the same scale so a car doesn't look inconsistently different between screens.

summary: MORE STATS: Left/Right switches car in place (1P stats screen)
detail: On the single-player MORE STATS screen, press Left/Right and confirm it switches which car you're viewing IN PLACE, rather than returning you to car-select.

summary: MORE STATS: captions under less-obvious bars; text clears RANDOM
detail: Confirm any stat bar whose meaning isn't obvious has a caption underneath; switch cars via RANDOM and confirm any leftover caption from the previous car is cleared, not overlapping.

summary: Menu nav: UP/DOWN/LEFT/RIGHT follow visual order, no skips
detail: Navigate any frontend menu with UP/DOWN/LEFT/RIGHT and confirm the highlight moves in the order things are actually laid out on screen, no skipped or visually-unrelated jumps.

summary: Menu nav: LEFT/RIGHT steps OK<->BACK on a GAMEPAD (no skip)
detail: On a gamepad, use LEFT/RIGHT between OK and BACK at the bottom of a menu -- it should step cleanly between just those two, without skipping BACK or landing elsewhere.

summary: Menu nav: selectors still cycle their value on LEFT/RIGHT
detail: On a value-selector row (e.g. difficulty/laps), confirm LEFT/RIGHT still cycles its VALUE, not repurposed to only move focus between rows.

summary: Track Select: DYNAMICS has arrows, sits above OK/BACK
detail: On Track Select, find the DYNAMICS (ARCADE/SIM) row and confirm it has visible left/right arrows and sits above OK/BACK without overlapping or crowding them.

summary: Track Select: option rows fit without overlap (cup too)
detail: On both Track Select and its Cup version, confirm every option row (traffic, cops, laps, dynamics, etc.) fits without any row overlapping another.

summary: Cop Chase RANDOM COP: cop drawn at race start, pick car normally
detail: Start a Cop Chase with RANDOM COP and confirm which player becomes the cop is decided at race start (not pre-assigned in the lobby), and the cop then picks a car through the normal flow.

summary: MORE STATS shows real carparam physics (1P + split)
detail: Check MORE STATS numbers against a car's actual physics parameters (not placeholder values) in both single-player and split-screen.

summary: Heavy cars hit harder/shove; light cars get flung
detail: Collide a heavy car into a light car and vice versa -- the heavy car should shove the light one aside with more force, while the light car gets flung further in the reverse case.

summary: Heavy cars climb hills slower; light cars climb easy
detail: Take a heavy and a light car up the same steep hill -- the heavy car should climb noticeably slower/more strained, the light car comparatively easily.

summary: Power-to-weight: lighter cars out-accelerate heavy
detail: Compare acceleration between a lighter and a heavier car with similar power -- the lighter car should out-accelerate, reflecting real power-to-weight rather than weight-independent acceleration.

summary: Slipstream boost when tucked behind another car
detail: Tuck in closely behind another car on a straight and watch your speed -- a noticeable slipstream/draft boost should apply while tucked in, fading if you pull away or overtake.

summary: Downforce: grippy cars stay planted in fast corners
detail: Take a high-grip car through a fast corner at speed and confirm it stays planted noticeably better than a low-grip car would in the same corner at the same speed.

summary: ARCADE mode: collisions punchier than SIM but controlled
detail: Compare the same collision in ARCADE vs SIMULATION dynamics -- Arcade should feel punchier but still controlled, not so exaggerated the car becomes uncontrollable or glitches.

summary: ARCADE pads grant NITRO/GHOST/WRECK/HAZARD power-ups
detail: In Arcade, grab item boxes repeatedly and confirm you see all four original power-up kinds over time: NITRO, GHOST, WRECK, and HAZARD.

summary: Power-up chip + timer shows in each player's pane
detail: In split-screen Arcade with multiple humans holding power-ups at once, confirm EACH player's own pane shows their own power-up chip/timer, not player 1's or a shared/incorrect one.

summary: GHOST cars pass through others (white shimmer aura)
detail: Grab GHOST and check your car's visual effect -- a white shimmering aura, and you should pass through other cars/traffic without colliding while it's active.

summary: HAZARD drop spins out the next car that touches it
detail: Drop a HAZARD (oil) power-up behind you and have another car drive over it -- that car should visibly spin out from touching it.

summary: SIMULATION = arcade grip/power + realistic gravity
detail: Compare SIMULATION against ARCADE -- SIMULATION should keep the same grip/power feel but use more realistic gravity (jumps/airborne moments should feel heavier than Arcade's floatier feel).

summary: ARCADE/SIM picked on track + multiplayer screens
detail: Confirm the ARCADE/SIMULATION choice is available and settable from both the single-player track-select screen and the multiplayer setup screens.

summary: Demo fires after 1 min idle on main menu
detail: Sit on the main menu without pressing anything for about 1 minute and confirm the attract-mode demo automatically kicks in.

summary: Demo: random track/car/opponents/traffic, no cops
detail: Watch an attract-mode demo and confirm it picks a random track, car, and opponents, includes normal traffic, but never spawns cops.

summary: Demo returns to main menu on finish (no results)
detail: Let a demo race run to completion and confirm it returns straight to the main menu, without ever showing a race-results screen.

summary: Cop Chase INFECT: arrested car becomes a random-car cop
detail: In INFECT mode, get a suspect arrested and confirm they immediately become a cop themselves, driving a randomly-chosen car, not necessarily the arresting cop's or their old suspect car.

summary: INFECT: human keeps driving as cop; AI cop gives chase
detail: After a human suspect is infected in INFECT mode, confirm they keep driving under their own control as the new cop, and AI-controlled cop(s) actually give chase, not idle.

summary: INFECT: round ends once every suspect is infected
detail: Play an INFECT round through and confirm it ends exactly when every original suspect has been turned into a cop, not on a fixed timer or lap count.

summary: Collisions fire on real model contact (mesh hitbox)
detail: Clip another car at an angle where the visual models actually touch but a simple bounding box might not (or vice versa) -- collisions should fire on the real model/mesh hitbox.

summary: Cop Chase results: COPS (arrests) + SUSPECTS (bust time)
detail: Finish a Cop Chase race and check results -- cops should be scored by arrest count and suspects by bust-survival time, as two distinct categories, not one shared column.

summary: Cop Chase ends: all suspects busted or all finish
detail: Play Cop Chase to completion and confirm it only ends when EITHER every suspect is busted OR every suspect finishes, not some other condition like a time limit.

summary: Italy (Courmayeur) starts a race without crashing
detail: Start any race type on Courmayeur (Italy) and confirm it loads and races normally without crashing -- previously an out-of-bounds route-table crash specific to this track's launch.

summary: Cop roles: OK rejected if all-cop or all-suspect
detail: On the Cop Chase role-assignment screen, try to confirm a setup where every player is a cop, and separately all-suspect -- both should be REJECTED since a valid chase needs at least one of each role.

summary: Arrested car stops dead, can't be driven/pushed
detail: Get a suspect arrested and try to drive or push that car -- it should be completely immobile: no throttle response and not nudgeable by other cars.

summary: ARRESTED splash centred; its floating bar hidden
detail: When a suspect is arrested, confirm the ARRESTED splash appears centred, and any floating status bar that car normally has is hidden rather than still showing.

summary: Arrested cars drop off the split-screen overview map
detail: In split-screen Cop Chase, arrest a suspect and check the overview/minimap -- their icon should disappear rather than continuing to be tracked.

summary: Strong short rumble for BOTH cop+suspect on arrest
detail: With both a cop's and a suspect's controllers connected during an arrest, confirm BOTH get a strong but short rumble pulse at the moment of arrest, not just one side.

summary: All suspects show a bust bar; shrinks w/ distance, 2x range
detail: As a cop, look at multiple suspects -- each should show its own bust-progress bar, shrinking as the suspect gets further away, with roughly double the effective range of an earlier version of this bar.

summary: CHOOSE YOUR TEAM / COP ROLES show profile names
detail: On both the CHOOSE YOUR TEAM (cup) and COP ROLES (cop chase) screens, confirm every player shows their actual profile name, not a generic PLAYER N placeholder.

summary: Cup team mode: host assigns AI opponents' teams
detail: As host, set up a team-mode cup and confirm you can assign which team each AI opponent belongs to, not just the human players.

summary: Cup team mode: per-opponent skill slider drives AI
detail: In cup team-mode setup, adjust an individual AI opponent's skill slider and race them -- their driving pace should visibly reflect that setting.

summary: Dev: host assigns teams to ADD AI PLAYER bots
detail: Using the dev ADD AI PLAYER tool, confirm the host can also assign added bots to specific teams in a team-mode cup, the same as real AI opponents.

summary: Cup final standings show profile names (not PLAYER N)
detail: Finish a full cup and check the final standings -- every entrant should be listed by their actual profile name, not a generic PLAYER N label.

summary: WHAT NEXT? CAR SELECTION -> car grid (not profile select)
detail: After a cup race, choose CAR SELECTION from WHAT NEXT? and confirm it goes straight to the car-grid picker, not a profile-select screen first.

summary: WHAT NEXT? buttons aligned under the title (both layouts)
detail: Open WHAT NEXT? in both single-player and split-screen/MP layouts and confirm the buttons align consistently under the title in both, not shifted in one.

summary: Crisp on 4K/high-DPI scaled displays (no blur)
detail: Run the game on a 4K or high-DPI/scaled display and check menu text/UI for blur -- everything should render crisp at that scaling, not soft or upscaled.

summary: Menu UP/LEFT work with 5+ pads (no stuck-stick block)
detail: Connect 5+ gamepads and try UP/LEFT from any one of them -- navigation should work normally; previously that many pads could leave UP/LEFT effectively stuck/blocked.

summary: Rumble survives many races (no FF death after first race)
detail: Play several races in a row on the same session and confirm force-feedback keeps working on every one -- previously it could die after the first race and never return.

summary: Pad that sleeps/reconnects mid-session re-rumbles next race
detail: Let a wireless pad sleep (or disconnect/reconnect) mid-session, then start the next race -- rumble should resume, not stay permanently dead.

summary: MP results table: name aligned, columns, divider
detail: Finish an MP race with several players and check results -- names aligned, values in clean columns, and a visible divider separating header from rows.

summary: Cup results screen + POINTS column (race + total)
detail: Finish a cup race and check the results screen for a POINTS column showing both this race's points and the running cup total.

summary: 7-player empty cells: one MAP + one STANDINGS (no dup)

summary: Live-control MCP socket drives a running dev td5re.exe (needs claude3 — game launch)
detail: [MCP CONTROL 2026-07-21] New dev-only, opt-in UDP command socket (td5_control.c, 127.0.0.1:37060, [Control] Enabled=1 / --Control=1, default OFF, TD5RE_CONTROL_PORT override) + Python MCP server (scripts/td5re_mcp/). claude2 verified build-only (DEV+RELEASE clean; structure-lint ratchets unchanged extern_in_c=3/game_h_includers=24) but CANNOT run the game. Full end-to-end test is in scripts/td5re_mcp/TESTING_HANDOFF.md — run on claude3: (1) launch_game() -> MENU + ping returns proto:1/build:dev; (2) start_race(track=5,opponents=3,cops=1) -> get_state RACE with correct track/mode -> screenshot -> end_race -> back to MENU; repeat with circuit/TD6/drag/arcade game_type variants + abort_current:true mid-race relaunch; (3) set_screen walk, set_param('trace_fast_forward',4)=next_race / debug_overlay=immediate, tap_key nav; (4) REGRESSION: full selftest suite still green + goldens byte-identical with the socket OFF (module must not perturb the idle sim); (5) RELEASE build opens NO socket on 37060 (--Control=1 silently ignored, compiled out). Known v1 gaps: get_state race info is track/mode/tick/actor-count/player-slot only (no per-racer pos/lap/speed); hold/tap drive keyboard DIK (no per-slot race-action bits); screenshot inherits the desktop black-capture gotcha (prefer TD5RE_FRAMEDUMP). [TESTED 2026-07-21 claude3: end-to-end PASS. Smoke ping proto:1/build:dev + MENU; races base(trk5,opp3,cop1)/circuit(trk5)/TD6(trk26)/drag(trk19,gt9)/arcade(trk2,dyn0,trf1,cop1) all reached RACE with correct track/mode + real (1-3 MB) screenshots + clean end_race->MENU; abort_current mid-race relaunch trk5->trk2 returned pending:true then raced trk2; set_screen 21/12/23 matched; list_params=20 knobs, trace_fast_forward->next_race, debug_overlay->immediate, unknown->ok:false, clamp opp 99->5; tap_key nav logged; quit exit 0. REGRESSION: full selftest 49/49 exit 0 with socket OFF, goldens byte-identical (moscow+pelton 7-hash + 4-frame). RELEASE td5re_release.exe --Control=1 opened NO socket on 37060 (silently ignored). Two findings filed as separate rows below.]

summary: game_client.py crashed on ConnectionResetError during launch/boot-wait (FIXED)
detail: [MCP CONTROL finding 2026-07-21 claude3] On Windows, sending a UDP datagram to port 37060 before the game has bound it returns an ICMP port-unreachable, surfaced by recvfrom as WSAECONNRESET (WinError 10054). game_client.GameClient.command() only caught socket.timeout, so the very first ping during the launch/boot wait raised an unhandled ConnectionResetError and aborted the whole driver instead of retrying. FIX APPLIED: command() now also catches ConnectionResetError (treat as no-listener-yet, 0.1 s backoff + retry within the existing retries loop); added import time. Re-verified: launch_game boot-wait now polls cleanly until the socket answers. Confirm the fix holds if server.py is driven via MCP (same client path).

summary: start_race sticky game_type leaks across races -> hard crash (drag mode + traffic/cops on a non-drag track)
detail: [MCP CONTROL finding 2026-07-21 claude3] start_race/cs_apply only overwrite the scenario fields the caller supplies (documented "unset fields keep their INI value"). Consequence: after a drag race (game_type=9), a follow-up start_race(track=2, traffic=1, cops=1) that omits game_type inherits game_type=9, so the engine loads the drag strip (level030.zip) for track_index=2, runs the drag lengthen/widen path, then places traffic+cops slots on that mangled strip and CRASHES: 0xC0000005 at td5re.exe+0x3E731 (access=300aa16a), engine.log shows repeated "level_number: drag race (game_type=9 mp_drag=0) -> level030.zip" for track_index=2. Not a control-transport defect (the module faithfully applied the leaked config) but a sharp MCP footgun + a latent engine crash for the drag-mode + traffic/cops + non-drag-track combo. RECOMMEND one of: (a) start_race resets mode-defining fields (game_type/dynamics) to an explicit default unless supplied; (b) the engine rejects/guards game_type=9 with traffic/cops on a non-drag track instead of dereferencing; (c) at minimum document the sticky-field semantics in server.py/handoff. Workaround today: always pass game_type explicitly. Verified arcade case passes once game_type=0 is passed. [ROOT CAUSE CONFIRMED 2026-07-21 claude3 via addr2line/nm on the unstripped DEV exe: crash is a USE-AFTER-FREE in td5_ai_refresh_route_state_slot (td5_ai.c:1650, EIP td5re.exe+0x3E731) reached from td5_ai_set_route_tables. Chain: td5_track_load_routes (td5_track.c:6848) on a level RELOAD frees the old s_route_left/right buffers (6852-6858 + drag-extend free(old) at 6916) then calls td5_ai_set_route_tables(new) at 6925 -> td5_ai_refresh_route_state (td5_ai.c:1239) loops every active slot -> refresh_route_state_slot PATH 2b (default, td5_ai.c:1571-1574) leaves rs[RS_ROUTE_TABLE_PTR] UNCHANGED = the PREVIOUS race buffer, just freed; line 1650 route_table[span*3+1] then derefs the freed ptr (0x300aa010). The index is bounds-clamped (1642-1647) but the BASE pointer is not validated. Latent because: normal levels leave the freed buffer mapped in the CRT heap (garbage, no fault) while the drag EXTEND path mallocs/frees LARGE buffers that get individually mapped+unmapped -> hard fault; needs two consecutive drag-level loads + traffic/cops raising active-slot count so a stale PATH-2b slot is processed; selftest never hits it (drag runs solo, arcade-tr-cops uses single-race game_type). SURGICAL FIX: in refresh_route_state_slot, after resolving route_table (line 1582), if route_table is neither g_route_tables[0] nor g_route_tables[1] it is stale -> resync rs[RS_ROUTE_TABLE_PTR]=g_route_tables[selector] (and route_table) before any deref. Preserves byte-faithful PATH 2b for the live-pointer case (goldens unaffected) and only intervenes cross-level. Optionally also add an upstream config guard rejecting game_type=9 with traffic|cops on a non-drag track. REQUIRES golden re-verify (td5_ai.c is golden-guarded). [FIX ATTEMPT 2026-07-21 claude3 — REVERTED, goldens broke: the naive equality guard (resync rs[RS_ROUTE_TABLE_PTR] when it != g_route_tables[0]/[1]) FAILS the golden suite (race-golden-moscow pose/motion + rgold L1 growing 2451->6041). Reason: the golden baseline DEPENDS on the premature refresh. In the selftest (many races in ONE process), when a golden race's level loads, td5_ai_set_route_tables -> td5_ai_refresh_route_state runs while rs[] still holds the PREVIOUS scenario's just-freed (but still-mapped) route ptr; the baseline derefs that stale-but-mapped buffer and writes garbage-derived rs fields (RS_FORWARD_TRACK_COMP, boundaries, g_actor_forward_track_component[]) that init_runtime does NOT fully overwrite, so the golden ENCODES the garbage-read initial state. The guard instead reads the new valid table -> different initial state -> chaotic sim divergence. Equality can't tell freed-stale from a legit table anyway (both are one of the two tables; per-tick code at td5_ai.c:3198 relies on rs==g_route_tables[0] to mean primary route). CORRECT FIX (golden-preserving, own /fix session): buffer-LIFETIME reorder in td5_track_load_routes (td5_track.c) — DEFER free() of the old s_route_left/right (and the drag-extend free(old) at 6916) until AFTER the new tables are installed + refreshed, instead of freeing at 6852-6858/6863 up front. The premature refresh then reads the SAME bytes (free doesn't zero freed memory, so golden-identical) but the page stays mapped so the drag large-buffer case can't fault. Touches golden-guarded td5_track.c -> full golden re-verify required. Root-cause investigation + failed-attempt evidence complete; implementation deferred.

summary: Golden harness does not pin [GameOptions] sim knobs - local td5re.ini edits silently break goldens
detail: [ROOT-CAUSED 2026-07-22] The 4 golden FAILs seen on 2026-07-21/22 (pose 01500e23abd0c189 vs 7cdf42f645bace29 etc.) were NOT a code regression: the local td5re.ini had Dynamics=0 (ARCADE), Difficulty=1, Powerups=2, CarToughness=2, CarDeform=2, CarDamage=1, Language=1 vs the committed defaults, and at least one of these leaks into the golden-race sim (suite is 50/50 with the committed INI, 46/4 with the edited one - verified both ways same build). Fix candidates: st_apply_scenario/golden setup should pin ALL sim-relevant [GameOptions] fields (dynamics, difficulty, powerups, toughness, deform, damage) the way it already pins track/car/laps; or selftest.ps1 should launch with explicit --Key overrides. Until fixed: before trusting a golden FAIL, diff td5re.ini vs git.

summary: rgold-pelton t130 is sensitive to pending_to_test.csv content even with the overlay OFF (uninitialized-read suspect)
detail: [FOUND 2026-07-22 while validating scenarios] With the pending overlay force-disabled before every selftest race (st_apply_scenario), the rgold-race-golden-pelton t130 render frame STILL flips between two clusters (worst-cell ~12-48 PASS vs ~86-122 FAIL) depending on the CSV's content: same build passes 50/50 with the committed CSV and fails t130 with a rewritten CSV (2 status flips + re-encoded lines). Sim/trace goldens match in all cases - render-only. Working theory: boot-time pending-list allocations shift the heap layout and some early-race TD6/pelton RENDER path reads uninitialized or stale memory whose content depends on that layout (same class as the drag route-table lesson: baseline depends on garbage). Investigate: capture t130 framedumps in both states and diff the affected 16x16 cells to locate the on-screen region, then hunt the uninitialized read. Until fixed, a lone rgold-pelton t130 FAIL right after editing pending_to_test.csv is environmental.
